title: Podman is extremely slow while pulling image on windows 10
body:
### Issue Description

When try to pull an image from a registry (e.g. JFrog), podman takes lot of time in copying blob steps.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run init command - podman machine init --cpus 2 --memory 2048 --disk-size 20 --user-mode-networking
2. podman machine set --rootful
3. podman machine start



### Describe the results you received

time="2023-09-11T22:02:32+05:30" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug"
time="2023-09-11T22:02:32+05:30" level=debug msg="Called pull.PersistentPreRunE(podman pull myjfrog.com/docker/java/openjdk:jre17.0.4_8 --log-level debug)"

(stuck here..........)

### Describe the results you expected

It should not stuck on copying blob steps for long time.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 10 Pro 22H2
4 Core, 16GB RAM
wsl --version
WSL version: 1.2.5.0
Kernel version: 5.15.90.1
WSLg version: 1.0.51
MSRDC version: 1.2.3770
Direct3D version: 1.608.2-61064218

Windows version: 10.0.19045.3208

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
@mtrmac @vrothberg PTAL
Could this be a network setup issue?

There’s literally nothing to go on here. What is the image, or at least its characteristics? Which aspect of the VM (CPU, memory, swap, disk I/O, network) is under load?

This requires generic Linux performance tuning expertise / documentation, first.
Seems to be VPN issue. VPN is slowing down downloading of images from our on-prem registry.  When I download from Docker hub it is much faster.
Thanks, @ravindrakmr.  I think we can close the issue since it's not related to Podman but the VPN.
